title: Open Data Standards Gain Traction Among Business Teams
summary: JSON Schema, Parquet awareness, and documented CSV conventions help teams swap tools without rework.

content.summary: JSON Schema, Parquet awareness, and documented CSV conventions help teams swap tools without rework.

body.text: Open formats reduce lock-in anxiety. Business teams notice when exports include consistent column names and ISO dates. Finance may still email Excel, but product and ops increasingly export JSON from internal tools. Standards make those files portable across analytics products.

body.alt: Business planning with documents and laptop
body.caption: Business planning with documents and laptop. Photo: Unsplash

body.text: Finance may still email Excel, but product and ops increasingly export JSON from internal tools. Standards make those files portable across analytics products.
Teams adopting naming conventions—snake_case columns, explicit currency fields—spend less time cleaning on every upload.

items: ISO 8601 dates prevent silent chart errors.
Documented enums make filters reliable across months.
JSON arrays flatten cleanly when schema is stable.

body.text: Analytics tools advertise broad format support and schema inference that respects documented types.
Standardization efforts work best when paired with preview screens that catch type mismatches immediately.
Datilio supports CSV, Excel, and JSON with preview-first workflows so standard formats deliver immediate value.

meta_description: JSON Schema, Parquet awareness, and documented CSV conventions help teams swap tools without rework.
